message:
feat(print-mode): stream text deltas to stdout and guard retries after visible output

Print mode (pi -p) previously buffered the entire response and
rendered it at the end. This adds real-time text streaming:

- Introduce PrintTextStreamState struct tracking whether text has
  been streamed and whether it ended with a newline
- In text mode, text deltas are written to stdout as they arrive
  via emit_text_delta(), providing immediate visual feedback
- If streaming already emitted text, the final render step is
  skipped to avoid duplication; otherwise falls back to the
  existing render-at-end path
- Add can_retry() guard: once text has been visibly streamed to
  the user, retrying would produce duplicated/garbled output,
  so retries are blocked (except in JSON mode where the format
  is machine-consumable)
- Add revert_last_user_message() call before retry to prevent
  context duplication when the provider fails before emitting
  an assistant message
- Add unit tests for streamed_text_delta() and
  PrintTextStreamState behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
